tasks.title: Update Drizzle schema: rename cancelledAt, add deletedAt, update index WHERE clauses
tasks.description:
Edit `packages/db/src/schemas/billing/billing_addon_purchase.dbschema.ts`:
1. Rename property `cancelledAt` to `canceledAt` (line ~42): `canceledAt: timestamp('canceled_at', { withTimezone: true }),`
2. Add new column after `updatedAt`: `deletedAt: timestamp('deleted_at', { withTimezone: true }),` (nullable, no default)
3. Update partial index WHERE clause on `addonPurchases_active_customer_idx` (line ~67): `sql\`status = 'active' AND deleted_at IS NULL\``
4. Update partial index WHERE clause on `idx_addon_purchases_active_unique` (line ~70): `sql\`status = 'active' AND deleted_at IS NULL\``

Test: Run `pnpm typecheck` from packages/db. Expect compile errors in downstream consumers (expected at this stage).

subtasks.title: Rename cancelledAt → canceledAt (column name becomes canceled_at)
Add deletedAt column (nullable timestamptz, no default)
Update addonPurchases_active_customer_idx WHERE to include AND deleted_at IS NULL
Update idx_addon_purchases_active_unique WHERE to include AND deleted_at IS NULL

tasks.title: Generate Drizzle migration, review SQL, and hand-edit as needed
tasks.description:
Run `pnpm db:generate` from the project root.

CRITICAL INTERACTIVE STEP: When drizzle-kit prompts 'Is column renamed?', answer YES for cancelled_at → canceled_at. Answering NO causes DROP+ADD which PERMANENTLY LOSES DATA.

After generation, review the migration SQL file in `packages/db/src/migrations/`:
1. Verify `RENAME COLUMN cancelled_at TO canceled_at` (NOT DROP + ADD)
2. Verify both partial indexes are DROPped and reCREATEd with `AND deleted_at IS NULL`
3. Check index WHERE clauses for literal values (not `$1` placeholders). Known drizzle-kit bugs #461, #4790 may produce broken SQL. Hand-edit using reference SQL from spec if needed.
4. Manually append at the end of the migration file:
   ```sql
   UPDATE billing_addon_purchases SET status = 'canceled' WHERE status = 'cancelled';
   ```

Review checklist:
- [ ] ADD COLUMN deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ present
- [ ] RENAME COLUMN (not DROP+ADD) present
- [ ] Both indexes DROP+CREATE with AND deleted_at IS NULL
- [ ] Index WHERE uses literal values, not $1
- [ ] Status data migration UPDATE appended at end

subtasks.title: Run pnpm db:generate and answer YES to rename prompt
Verify RENAME COLUMN (not DROP+ADD) in generated SQL
Verify partial index WHERE clauses use literal values (hand-edit if $1 placeholders)
Append UPDATE status data migration at end of file

tasks.title: Add new unit tests for soft-delete behavior
tasks.description:
Add new test cases (in existing test files or a new focused test file) covering soft-delete filtering:

1. **getUserAddons() excludes soft-deleted**: Create a mock addon purchase with `status: 'active'` AND `deletedAt: new Date()`. Assert that getUserAddons() does NOT return it.
2. **findExpiredAddons() excludes soft-deleted**: Create a mock expired addon purchase with `deletedAt: new Date()`. Assert findExpiredAddons() does NOT return it.
3. **getCustomerAddonAdjustments() excludes soft-deleted**: Create a mock active addon purchase with `deletedAt: new Date()`. Assert getCustomerAddonAdjustments() does NOT return it.
4. **Admin customer-addons list excludes soft-deleted by default**: Mock a soft-deleted record. Assert the admin list endpoint does NOT return it without `includeDeleted`.
5. **Unique index allows repurchase after soft-delete**: Verify that inserting a new active record for the same (customerId, addonSlug) pair succeeds when the prior active record is soft-deleted (deletedAt IS NOT NULL).

Each test should follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert). Use existing mock patterns from mock-factories.ts.

Test: Run `pnpm test` on the relevant test files. Coverage >= 90% on modified service files.

subtasks.title: Test: getUserAddons() excludes soft-deleted active record
Test: findExpiredAddons() excludes soft-deleted expired record
Test: getCustomerAddonAdjustments() excludes soft-deleted active record
Test: admin customer-addons list excludes soft-deleted by default
Test: unique index allows repurchase after soft-delete

tasks.title: Add test for cancelUserAddon status value consistency
tasks.description:
Add a unit test that verifies `cancelUserAddon()` writes `status: 'canceled'` (American, single-L) to the database.

Test case:
- Arrange: Set up mock for an active addon purchase.
- Act: Call cancelUserAddon() with the purchase ID.
- Assert: The Drizzle UPDATE .set() call was invoked with `{ status: 'canceled', canceledAt: expect.any(Date), updatedAt: expect.any(Date) }`.

This test ensures the British spelling `'cancelled'` is never written to the database again.

Test file: Add to `apps/api/test/services/addon.service.test.ts` or the relevant cancel test section.

subtasks.title: Write test: cancelUserAddon writes status 'canceled' (not 'cancelled')
Assert canceledAt (not cancelledAt) is set in UPDATE

tasks.title: Run full validation suite: typecheck + lint + test + grep verification
tasks.description:
Run the complete validation suite:

1. `pnpm typecheck` (entire monorepo) - must pass with zero errors.
2. `pnpm lint` (entire monorepo) - must pass with zero errors.
3. `pnpm test` (entire monorepo) - all tests pass, coverage >= 90% on modified files.
4. Grep verification:
   - `rg 'cancelledAt|cancelled_at' --type ts` → zero results in service/route/schema/test files (docs are OK).
   - `rg "'cancelled'" apps/api/src apps/admin/src` → zero results for addon-related code (subscription and MercadoPago acceptable).
   - `rg '"cancelled"|"statusCancelled"' packages/i18n/src/locales/*/admin-billing.json` → `"cancelled"` ONLY under sponsorships.statuses and subscriptions.statuses (not under addons), and zero `"statusCancelled"` matches.

If any check fails, identify and fix the issue before proceeding to T-021.

subtasks.title: Run pnpm typecheck - zero errors
Run pnpm lint - zero errors
Run pnpm test - all pass, coverage OK
Grep: zero cancelledAt/cancelled_at in TS files (except docs)
Grep: zero 'cancelled' in addon-related API/admin code
Grep: zero statusCancelled in i18n, cancelled only in sponsorship/subscription keys

tasks.title: Apply migration to dev database and verify
tasks.description:
Apply the migration to the development database:

1. Run `pnpm db:migrate`.
2. Verify columns: `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'billing_addon_purchases' ORDER BY ordinal_position;`
   - `deleted_at` must exist (nullable timestamptz)
   - `canceled_at` must exist (not `cancelled_at`)
3. Verify no British status remains: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM billing_addon_purchases WHERE status = 'cancelled';` → must return 0.
4. Verify existing data preserved: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM billing_addon_purchases WHERE canceled_at IS NOT NULL;` → should match pre-migration count of rows with cancellation timestamps.

This is the final step. After verification, update spec status from 'draft' to 'completed'.

subtasks.title: Run pnpm db:migrate
Verify deleted_at and canceled_at columns exist
Verify zero rows with status = 'cancelled'
Verify existing canceled_at timestamps preserved
Update spec status to completed
